title: The Abstinence

jokes.text: Who are you gonna eat to survive? -Kramer. -So fast? What about me? -No.
jokes.setup: Hypothetical plane crash survival scenario
jokes.punchline: Jerry immediately chooses Kramer over George without hesitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Jerry's casual dismissal of George is both cruel and funny, showing their friendship dynamic

jokes.text: Kramer's so stringy. I'm plump, juicy.
jokes.setup: George defending why he'd be a better choice to eat
jokes.punchline: George describes himself in food terms with disturbing enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: George's self-description as 'plump, juicy' is both disturbing and perfectly in character - his vanity even extends to cannibalism

jokes.text: Kramer's got more muscle, higher protein content. It's better for you.
jokes.setup: Continuing the cannibalism debate
jokes.punchline: Jerry treats eating his friends like a health-conscious consumer choice
jokes.explanation: Jerry's practical approach to cannibalism is absurd but logically consistent

jokes.text: If other people are having some, I'll try you.
jokes.setup: Jerry won't eat George, George says he'd eat Jerry
jokes.punchline: George treats eating Jerry like trying a new appetizer at a party
jokes.explanation: George's conditional cannibalism is perfectly petty and social

jokes.text: With me, or in general?
jokes.setup: Woman says she can't have sex
jokes.punchline: George asks for clarification in a self-deprecating way
jokes.explanation: Classic George self-deprecation delivered with perfect timing and economy

jokes.text: So how long is this not gonna be a problem for me?
jokes.setup: George learning about his girlfriend's mono diagnosis
jokes.punchline: George frames her medical condition in terms of his own convenience
jokes.explanation: Perfect George selfishness - turning her illness into his inconvenience

jokes.text: I'm like a sexual camel.
jokes.setup: George claiming six weeks without sex is no problem
jokes.punchline: George compares himself to a camel in terms of sexual endurance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor that's both absurd and memorable - perfectly quotable George line

jokes.text: No, it's not debunk. It's totally bunk. -Isn't bunk bad? Like, 'that's a lot of bunk'? -No, something is bunk, then you debunk it.
jokes.setup: Discussion about dating doctors being debunked
jokes.punchline: Characters get confused about the meaning of 'bunk' vs 'debunk'
jokes.explanation: Typical Seinfeld wordplay but not particularly strong - feels forced

jokes.text: I've heard of Ohio, Katie
jokes.setup: Katie explains Dayton Civic Centre is in Ohio
jokes.punchline: Jerry's dry response treats this as condescending
jokes.explanation: Good Jerry sarcasm but fairly standard

jokes.text: And you were integral.
jokes.setup: George claiming he works for a World Series-winning team
jokes.punchline: Jerry sarcastically calls George's contribution 'integral'
jokes.explanation: Nice put-down of George's minimal role, delivered with perfect sarcasm

jokes.text: I'm getting bumped? You're bumping me from Career Day?
jokes.setup: Jerry learns the lizard guy went long
jokes.punchline: Jerry's indignation at being bumped from a junior high career day
jokes.explanation: Jerry's wounded pride over something so minor is perfectly petty

jokes.text: Then I'm not really attracted you.
jokes.setup: Ben admits he's not really a doctor
jokes.punchline: Elaine immediately loses attraction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Elaine shallowness - her attraction was purely about status

jokes.text: You got bumped from Career Day?
jokes.setup: Jerry explains he didn't get to speak
jokes.punchline: George's disbelief that Jerry could be bumped from something so minor
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces the absurdity of Jerry's situation

jokes.text: Why does Radio Shack ask for your phone number when you buy batteries?
jokes.setup: Jerry questions why junior high would mess with his head
jokes.punchline: Kramer responds with unrelated corporate paranoia
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer non-sequitur that's both brilliant and totally in character - became a cultural reference

jokes.text: You can make all the laws you want. He's still gonna bother people.
jokes.setup: Larry says smoking bothers people and it's illegal
jokes.punchline: Jerry's fatalistic view that Kramer will bother people regardless
jokes.explanation: Good Jerry observation about Kramer's inherent nature

jokes.text: Like fire in a school is such a big deal.
jokes.setup: Fire drill interrupts Jerry's career day slot
jokes.punchline: Jerry dismisses fire safety in schools
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that reveals Jerry's self-centeredness

jokes.text: Before you know it, Letterman won't return your calls.
jokes.setup: Katie warns about the slippery slope of being bumped
jokes.punchline: Career Day bump leads to Letterman rejection
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the career day situation to absurd extremes

jokes.text: I just remembered where I left my retainer in second grade.
jokes.setup: Jerry explains George's brain is now functioning without sexual distraction
jokes.punchline: George suddenly remembers an extremely specific childhood detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of George's newfound mental clarity - absurd but believable

jokes.text: Just because a person's a smoker doesn't mean he's not a human being. -It doesn't?
jokes.setup: Kramer defends smokers' humanity
jokes.punchline: Jerry questions even this basic assertion
jokes.explanation: Jerry's deadpan response undercuts Kramer's noble sentiment perfectly

jokes.text: These people aren't gonna let themselves be flicked into the ashbin of society. -Why not?
jokes.setup: Kramer uses 'ashbin' metaphor for smokers being ostracized
jokes.punchline: Jerry asks why they wouldn't just accept it
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with 'ashbin' and Jerry's practical response

jokes.text: Dead bodies really gross me out.
jokes.setup: Elaine asks about choosing cadavers in medical school
jokes.punchline: The medical student is squeamish about dead bodies
jokes.explanation: Ironic that someone in medical school would be grossed out by the core requirement

jokes.text: Rick is a periodontist. He does Giuliani's gums.
jokes.setup: Sue Ellen introducing her boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about treating a public figure's gums
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from the specificity but not particularly strong

jokes.text: Can't you at least tell them what to do? -Like what? -Shouldn't he elevate his legs? -Right. Elevate your legs.
jokes.setup: Someone passes out, people expect Ben to help as a 'doctor'
jokes.punchline: Ben just repeats Elaine's suggestion as medical advice
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment as Ben's fake expertise is exposed

jokes.text: I call him doctor.
jokes.setup: Sue Ellen questioning if Ben is really a doctor
jokes.punchline: Elaine's weak defense is just that she calls him doctor
jokes.explanation: Elaine's desperation to maintain the facade is mildly funny

jokes.text: You know, they come in once, it's like they're addicted.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking about Kramer's smokers' lounge
jokes.punchline: Kramer doesn't realize he's describing actual addiction
jokes.explanation: Kramer's obliviousness to smoking addiction is perfectly in character

jokes.text: They cancelled Rick James. -'Super Freak'? -Yes.
jokes.setup: Katie got Jerry an assembly slot
jokes.punchline: Jerry was chosen over Rick James for a junior high assembly
jokes.explanation: Absurd that a comedian would replace a famous musician at a school

jokes.text: Oh, yes, you are. We'll just stop having sex.
jokes.setup: Ben says he'll never pass his medical exam
jokes.punchline: Elaine proposes sexual abstinence as the solution
jokes.explanation: Good callback to George's situation with logical extension to Elaine's problem

jokes.text: Are you the guy who put us in that Ramada in Milwaukee?
jokes.setup: George introducing himself to players as baseball expert
jokes.punchline: Player recognizes him for hotel screw-up instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment - his attempt at expertise backfires into recognition for incompetence

jokes.text: In six games.
jokes.setup: Player mentions they won the World Series
jokes.punchline: George criticizes them for not sweeping
jokes.explanation: Brilliant George arrogance - criticizing World Series winners for not being efficient enough. Peak character comedy.

jokes.text: Nine minutes.
jokes.setup: Jerry finishes his career presentation
jokes.punchline: Jerry realizes he only killed 9 minutes of a 2-hour slot
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the setup about Jerry having to fill two hours

jokes.text: And give you the chance to see some real disappointed kids.
jokes.setup: George offers to help fill time at the assembly
jokes.punchline: Jerry predicts the kids will be disappointed by George
jokes.explanation: Jerry's realistic assessment of George's appeal to children

jokes.text: Portuguese? Yeah, my cleaning lady is Portuguese. I must have picked it up.
jokes.setup: Waitress detects Portuguese accent in George
jokes.punchline: George claims he absorbed accent from his cleaning lady
jokes.explanation: Absurd explanation that's somehow believable for super-smart George

jokes.text: I stopped having sex with Ben three days ago and I don't know no Portuguese.
jokes.setup: Discussion of George getting smarter without sex
jokes.punchline: Elaine's double negative proves she's getting dumber
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of Elaine getting dumber - the grammar mistake proves the point

jokes.text: To a woman, sex is like the garbage man.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining why abstinence affects men and women differently
jokes.punchline: Comparing women's relationship to sex to garbage collection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant and somewhat controversial metaphor that became a memorable Seinfeld observation

jokes.text: The bags are piling up in your head. The sidewalk is blocked. Nothing's getting through. You're stupid.
jokes.setup: Continuing the garbage strike metaphor
jokes.punchline: Jerry extends metaphor to explain Elaine's declining intelligence
jokes.explanation: Good extension of the metaphor with brutal conclusion

jokes.text: It looks like an old catcher's mitt.
jokes.setup: Jerry noticing Kramer's face after smoking
jokes.punchline: Comparing Kramer's weathered face to sports equipment
jokes.explanation: Good visual metaphor for Kramer's smoking damage

jokes.text: You've experienced a lifetime of smoking in 72 hours.
jokes.setup: Kramer's face is damaged from smoking
jokes.punchline: Jerry explains the accelerated aging effect
jokes.explanation: Good explanation for the visual gag

jokes.text: Emphysema, birth defects, cancer, but not this.
jokes.setup: Jerry saying Kramer should have expected the damage
jokes.punchline: Kramer was prepared for serious diseases but not facial damage
jokes.explanation: Kramer's vanity outweighs concern for life-threatening diseases

jokes.text: Everything I have I owe to this face. It's my allure, my twinkle.
jokes.setup: Kramer lamenting his damaged appearance
jokes.punchline: Kramer attributing all his success to his looks
jokes.explanation: Kramer's delusions about his attractiveness are perfectly in character

jokes.text: 'Winnie-the-' blank. -Pooh. -Poo.
jokes.setup: Ben asks for crossword help
jokes.punchline: Elaine gets the simple answer wrong
jokes.explanation: Simple demonstration of Elaine's declining intelligence

jokes.text: Perhaps I can better serve the world this way.
jokes.setup: George considering permanent abstinence
jokes.punchline: George frames sexual abstinence as service to humanity
jokes.explanation: George's grandiose self-importance is perfectly captured

jokes.text: You mean, not subjecting women to your sexual advances?
jokes.setup: George claiming he's serving the world
jokes.punchline: Jerry interprets this as sparing women from George
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry deflation of George's pompousness

jokes.text: There was a pretty good chance I was never gonna have sex again anyway.
jokes.setup: Discussion of George giving up sex permanently
jokes.punchline: George admits his prospects were already terrible
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest George self-assessment that's both sad and funny

jokes.text: I wrote a 20-minute bit about how homework stinks.
jokes.setup: George prepared science experiments for kids
jokes.punchline: Jerry's pedestrian approach to entertaining children
jokes.explanation: Jerry's basic approach contrasts with George's intellectual pretensions

jokes.text: Oh, my vacation was restful, resplendent, magnificent. In fact, next time I'm planning on going to Corfu.
jokes.setup: George's newfound intelligence changing his speech patterns
jokes.punchline: George using pretentious vocabulary about vacation plans
jokes.explanation: Good demonstration of George's intellectual pretensions

jokes.text: Your face is my case.
jokes.setup: Kramer asking if they have a case against tobacco companies
jokes.punchline: Jackie's dramatic declaration about Kramer's face
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jackie Chiles line - dramatic, alliterative, and quotable

jokes.text: You could read the paper through the whole thing, if you want.
jokes.setup: George propositioning Elaine for casual sex
jokes.punchline: George offers to make it as impersonal as possible
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward George proposition that removes all romance

jokes.text: Is Kramer home?
jokes.setup: Elaine rejecting George's proposition
jokes.punchline: Elaine immediately considers Kramer as alternative
jokes.explanation: Brutal rejection of George followed by immediate pivot to Kramer

jokes.text: I used to share that very same outlook, but now I have so many things to occupy my mind. For instance, the atom.
jokes.setup: Louise says she needs to have sex
jokes.punchline: George pompously dismisses sex for intellectual pursuits
jokes.explanation: George's pretentious response with 'the atom' as his example of high thinking

jokes.text: In fact, I feel Mr. Kramer projects a rugged masculinity.
jokes.setup: Jackie calls Kramer a horrible twisted freak
jokes.punchline: Lawyer describes obviously damaged Kramer as ruggedly masculine
jokes.explanation: Good spin from the lawyer trying to make Kramer's damage seem appealing

jokes.text: By the time this case gets to trial, he'll be nothing more than a shrunken head.
jokes.setup: Kramer has only been smoking for four days
jokes.punchline: Jackie predicts Kramer will deteriorate to a shrunken head
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Kramer's deterioration with vivid imagery. Classic Jackie Chiles hyperbole.

jokes.text: I always knew that after I became a doctor I would dump whoever I was with and find someone better. That's the dream of becoming a doctor.
jokes.setup: Ben breaking up with Elaine after passing his exam
jokes.punchline: Ben admits this was always his plan and it's universal doctor behavior
jokes.explanation: Dark but honest take on medical profession motivations. Subverts the noble doctor trope.

jokes.text: I calculated my odds of ever getting together with a Portuguese waitress. Mathematically, I had to do it, Jerry.
jokes.setup: George explaining why he had sex with the waitress
jokes.punchline: George frames sexual decision as mathematical calculation
jokes.explanation: Perfect blend of smart George and regular George - using intelligence to justify sexual impulse

jokes.text: Hey, kids, what's the deal with homework? You're not working on your home.
jokes.setup: Jerry performing for the school assembly
jokes.punchline: Jerry's weak observational joke about homework
jokes.explanation: Intentionally weak joke showing Jerry's material falling flat with kids

jokes.text: A long time ago, my uncle and a date are driving, like, through Mexico. They see one on the road. Drags him out of the car and chews his face off.
jokes.setup: Executive explaining monitor lizards are dangerous
jokes.punchline: Absurd story about lizard attack in Mexico
jokes.explanation: Absurd justification for Jerry being bumped by a lizard, but feels somewhat forced

tmdb_overview: George's intellect thrives when thoughts of sex are shut out; Elaine wants her intern to become a doctor.
